content:
# Identity Verification Policy Overview

## When Verification Is Required
Confirm identity before reading account details or making any account change. Ask only for the information needed to reach the required match count, and pause the request if the customer cannot provide enough information. For higher-risk workflows, use the separate guidance in "Internal: High Risk Action Verification Matrix" before continuing.

## Accepted Customer Identifiers
The approved identity attributes are address, email, phone_number, date_of_birth. Do not treat other customer statements as substitutes for these attributes, even if they sound persuasive or match public information. If the customer gives partial information, ask a focused follow-up for one approved attribute instead of collecting unrelated facts.

## Matching Customer Responses
1. Compare name, email, address without requiring the same capitalization.
2. Normalize phone entries with digits_only before deciding whether they match.
3. Accept dates only in MM/DD/YYYY format.
4. If a supplied value conflicts with the customer record, count it as a non-match for that attribute and move to another approved attribute when available.

## Minimum Match Rule
Standard verification requires at least 2 matching approved attributes before account data is disclosed or changed. A repeated answer does not create an additional match; each match must come from a different approved attribute. See "Verification Matching Faq" for examples of formatting questions and common mismatch scenarios.
title: Identity Verification Policy Overview
